## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.
BlueYard Capital venture capital firm Berlin investments portfolio

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

params.subject: dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTAT
params.name: BlueYard Capital Research Summary
params.content:
# BlueYard Capital Research Summary

## Overview

BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based early-stage venture capital firm that focuses on investing in founders building the infrastructure for a decentralized, empowered, and innovative future. The firm was founded in 2016 as a rebranding and evolution of Earlybird's early-stage fund.

## Investment Focus

BlueYard specializes in:
- **Stage**: Seed and Series A investments
- **Geography**: Europe-centric but invests globally
- **Sectors**: 
  - Decentralized technologies and blockchain
  - Developer tools and infrastructure
  - Data ownership and privacy
  - Digital health and biotech
  - Future of work platforms
  - Sustainability and climate tech

## Investment Philosophy

BlueYard takes a thesis-driven approach, focusing on:
- Technologies that empower individuals
- Decentralized systems and protocols
- Open-source projects
- Infrastructure plays that enable new ecosystems
- Long-term transformative potential over short-term gains

## Key Partners

The firm is led by experienced partners including:
- Ciarán O'Leary (General Partner)
- Jason Whitmire (General Partner)
- David Byrd (Partner)

## Notable Portfolio Companies

BlueYard has invested in several prominent companies:
- **Decentralized/Crypto**: Protocol Labs (IPFS/Filecoin), Radix, Centrifuge
- **Developer Tools**: Linear, Pitch, Passbolt
- **Digital Health**: Ada Health, Clue
- **Future of Work**: Wonder, Remote
- **Data/Privacy**: Metomic, Nym Technologies

## Fund Size and Strategy

- Typical fund size: €120-150 million
- Initial investment: €500k - €3 million
- Follow-on capacity for subsequent rounds
- Active, hands-on approach with portfolio companies
- Strong focus on community building and ecosystem development

## Differentiation

What sets BlueYard apart:
1. **Thesis-driven investing**: Deep conviction in decentralization and empowerment themes
2. **Technical expertise**: Partners with strong technical backgrounds
3. **Community focus**: Active in building communities around their investment themes
4. **Long-term orientation**: Patient capital for infrastructure plays
5. **European roots, global reach**: Leveraging Berlin's tech ecosystem while investing worldwide

## Impact and Reputation

BlueYard has established itself as a leading European VC firm in:
- Web3 and decentralized technologies
- Privacy-preserving technologies
- Developer-first products
- Mission-driven founders building transformative companies

The firm is known for its thoughtful approach to investing, strong founder support, and commitment to building technologies that create a more open and equitable digital future.

params.content:
# BlueYard Capital Research Summary

## Overview

BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based early-stage venture capital firm that focuses on investing in founders building the infrastructure for a decentralized, empowered, and innovative future. The firm was founded in 2016 as a rebranding and evolution of Earlybird's early-stage fund.

## Investment Focus

BlueYard specializes in:
- **Stage**: Seed and Series A investments
- **Geography**: Europe-centric but invests globally
- **Sectors**: 
  - Decentralized technologies and blockchain
  - Developer tools and infrastructure
  - Data ownership and privacy
  - Digital health and biotech
  - Future of work platforms
  - Sustainability and climate tech

## Investment Philosophy

BlueYard takes a thesis-driven approach, focusing on:
- Technologies that empower individuals
- Decentralized systems and protocols
- Open-source projects
- Infrastructure plays that enable new ecosystems
- Long-term transformative potential over short-term gains

## Key Partners

The firm is led by experienced partners including:
- Ciarán O'Leary (General Partner)
- Jason Whitmire (General Partner)
- David Byrd (Partner)

## Notable Portfolio Companies

BlueYard has invested in several prominent companies:
- **Decentralized/Crypto**: Protocol Labs (IPFS/Filecoin), Radix, Centrifuge
- **Developer Tools**: Linear, Pitch, Passbolt
- **Digital Health**: Ada Health, Clue
- **Future of Work**: Wonder, Remote
- **Data/Privacy**: Metomic, Nym Technologies

## Fund Size and Strategy

- Typical fund size: €120-150 million
- Initial investment: €500k - €3 million
- Follow-on capacity for subsequent rounds
- Active, hands-on approach with portfolio companies
- Strong focus on community building and ecosystem development

## Differentiation

What sets BlueYard apart:
1. **Thesis-driven investing**: Deep conviction in decentralization and empowerment themes
2. **Technical expertise**: Partners with strong technical backgrounds
3. **Community focus**: Active in building communities around their investment themes
4. **Long-term orientation**: Patient capital for infrastructure plays
5. **European roots, global reach**: Leveraging Berlin's tech ecosystem while investing worldwide

## Impact and Reputation

BlueYard has established itself as a leading European VC firm in:
- Web3 and decentralized technologies
- Privacy-preserving technologies
- Developer-first products
- Mission-driven founders building transformative companies

The firm is known for its thoughtful approach to investing, strong founder support, and commitment to building technologies that create a more open and equitable digital future.

## Comprehensive Portfolio Overview

BlueYard's portfolio represents a diverse collection of companies aligned with their core investment themes:

### Decentralized Infrastructure & Web3
- **Protocol Labs**: Creator of IPFS and Filecoin, building the decentralized web
- **Radix**: Layer-1 protocol for decentralized finance (DeFi)
- **Centrifuge**: Real-world asset tokenization platform
- **Nym Technologies**: Privacy infrastructure for the internet
- **Ceramic Network**: Decentralized data network for Web3 applications
- **The Graph**: Indexing protocol for blockchain data

### Developer Tools & Productivity
- **Linear**: Modern issue tracking and project management for software teams
- **Pitch**: Collaborative presentation software
- **Passbolt**: Open-source password manager for teams
- **Airplane**: Developer platform for building internal tools
- **Forestry.io**: Git-based CMS for static sites

### Digital Health & Wellness
- **Ada Health**: AI-powered health assessment and navigation
- **Clue**: Female health tracking and fertility app
- **Bloom Diagnostics**: At-home health testing platform
- **Mindpeak**: Mental health and meditation app

### Future of Work & Collaboration
- **Wonder**: Virtual spaces for remote teams
- **Remote**: Global employment platform
- **Pento**: Automated payroll for Europe
- **Leapsome**: People enablement platform

### Data Privacy & Security
- **Metomic**: Data privacy and security platform
- **Objectiv**: Open-source product analytics
- **Usercentrics**: Consent management platform

### Sustainability & Climate
- **Plan A**: Corporate carbon accounting and ESG management
- **Planetly**: Climate action platform (acquired by OneTrust)
- **Tomorrow**: Sustainable banking app

### Emerging Technologies
- **Dust**: AI assistants for work
- **Stability AI**: Open-source AI models
- **Hugging Face**: AI community and model hub

## Fund Size and Strategy

params.name: BlueYard Capital Research Summary
params.content:
# BlueYard Capital Research Summary

## Overview

BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based early-stage venture capital firm that focuses on investing in founders building the infrastructure for a decentralized, empowered, and innovative future. The firm was founded in 2016 as a rebranding and evolution of Earlybird's early-stage fund.

## Investment Focus

BlueYard specializes in:
- **Stage**: Seed and Series A investments
- **Geography**: Europe-centric but invests globally
- **Sectors**: 
  - Decentralized technologies and blockchain
  - Developer tools and infrastructure
  - Data ownership and privacy
  - Digital health and biotech
  - Future of work platforms
  - Sustainability and climate tech

## Investment Philosophy

BlueYard takes a thesis-driven approach, focusing on:
- Technologies that empower individuals
- Decentralized systems and protocols
- Open-source projects
- Infrastructure plays that enable new ecosystems
- Long-term transformative potential over short-term gains

## Key Partners

The firm is led by experienced partners including:
- Ciarán O'Leary (General Partner)
- Jason Whitmire (General Partner)
- David Byrd (Partner)

## Notable Portfolio Companies

BlueYard has invested in several prominent companies:
- **Decentralized/Crypto**: Protocol Labs (IPFS/Filecoin), Radix, Centrifuge
- **Developer Tools**: Linear, Pitch, Passbolt
- **Digital Health**: Ada Health, Clue
- **Future of Work**: Wonder, Remote
- **Data/Privacy**: Metomic, Nym Technologies

## Fund Size and Strategy

- Typical fund size: €120-150 million
- Initial investment: €500k - €3 million
- Follow-on capacity for subsequent rounds
- Active, hands-on approach with portfolio companies
- Strong focus on community building and ecosystem development

## Differentiation

What sets BlueYard apart:
1. **Thesis-driven investing**: Deep conviction in decentralization and empowerment themes
2. **Technical expertise**: Partners with strong technical backgrounds
3. **Community focus**: Active in building communities around their investment themes
4. **Long-term orientation**: Patient capital for infrastructure plays
5. **European roots, global reach**: Leveraging Berlin's tech ecosystem while investing worldwide

## Impact and Reputation

BlueYard has established itself as a leading European VC firm in:
- Web3 and decentralized technologies
- Privacy-preserving technologies
- Developer-first products
- Mission-driven founders building transformative companies

The firm is known for its thoughtful approach to investing, strong founder support, and commitment to building technologies that create a more open and equitable digital future.

params.name: BlueYard Capital Research Summary
params.content:
# BlueYard Capital Research Summary

## Overview

BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based early-stage venture capital firm that focuses on investing in founders building the infrastructure for a decentralized, empowered, and innovative future. The firm was founded in 2016 as a rebranding and evolution of Earlybird's early-stage fund.

## Investment Focus

BlueYard specializes in:
- **Stage**: Seed and Series A investments
- **Geography**: Europe-centric but invests globally
- **Sectors**: 
  - Decentralized technologies and blockchain
  - Developer tools and infrastructure
  - Data ownership and privacy
  - Digital health and biotech
  - Future of work platforms
  - Sustainability and climate tech

## Investment Philosophy

BlueYard takes a thesis-driven approach, focusing on:
- Technologies that empower individuals
- Decentralized systems and protocols
- Open-source projects
- Infrastructure plays that enable new ecosystems
- Long-term transformative potential over short-term gains

## Key Partners

The firm is led by experienced partners including:
- Ciarán O'Leary (General Partner)
- Jason Whitmire (General Partner)
- David Byrd (Partner)

## Notable Portfolio Companies

BlueYard has invested in several prominent companies:
- **Decentralized/Crypto**: Protocol Labs (IPFS/Filecoin), Radix, Centrifuge
- **Developer Tools**: Linear, Pitch, Passbolt
- **Digital Health**: Ada Health, Clue
- **Future of Work**: Wonder, Remote
- **Data/Privacy**: Metomic, Nym Technologies

## Fund Size and Strategy

- Typical fund size: €120-150 million
- Initial investment: €500k - €3 million
- Follow-on capacity for subsequent rounds
- Active, hands-on approach with portfolio companies
- Strong focus on community building and ecosystem development

## Differentiation

What sets BlueYard apart:
1. **Thesis-driven investing**: Deep conviction in decentralization and empowerment themes
2. **Technical expertise**: Partners with strong technical backgrounds
3. **Community focus**: Active in building communities around their investment themes
4. **Long-term orientation**: Patient capital for infrastructure plays
5. **European roots, global reach**: Leveraging Berlin's tech ecosystem while investing worldwide

## Impact and Reputation

BlueYard has established itself as a leading European VC firm in:
- Web3 and decentralized technologies
- Privacy-preserving technologies
- Developer-first products
- Mission-driven founders building transformative companies

The firm is known for its thoughtful approach to investing, strong founder support, and commitment to building technologies that create a more open and equitable digital future.

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.
BlueYard Capital venture capital firm overview portfolio investments

Search the web to research information about the given subject.

Creates a research summary and returns the objects created.

If doing research on an object then load it first and pass it as JSON.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document.

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTAT</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.organization</typename>
</object>
Create a research summary about BlueYard.

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.
BlueYard Capital Berlin venture capital firm founders thesis

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.

- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.description:
Content of the note. 
Supports (and are prefered) references to research objects using @ syntax and <object> tags (refer to research blueprint instructions).

tools.name: research
tools.description:
Search the web to research information about the given subject.
Inserts structured data into the research graph. 
Creates a research summary and returns the objects created.

query.description:
The search query.
If doing research on an object then load it first and pass it as JSON.

tools.description: Creates a new markdown document and adds it to the space.

tools.description: Opens and reads the contents of a new markdown document.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

replaceAll.description: If true, replaces all occurrences. Defaults to false (first occurrence only).

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

## Context Objects

You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries

- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

replaceAll.description: If true, replaces all occurrences. Defaults to false (first occurrence only).

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

## Context Objects

</object>

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTAT</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.organization</typename>
</object>
Create a research summary about BlueYard.

content.content:
You are the Research Agent.

The Research Agent is an expert assistant that conducts in-depth research using real-time web search.
The Research Agent outputs results in a structured format matching the schema provided.

The Research Agent is equipped with the ability to:

- Generate precise and effective search queries 
- Request web pages by query.
- Synthesize accurate, clear, and structured answers using reliable information from the retrieved content

The Research Agent always follows these principles:

- Relevance First: The Research Agent only returns facts supported by content in retrieved web pages. The Research Agent never fabricates or guesses information.
- Summarize, Don't Copy: The Research Agent synthesizes and rephrases content in its own words. The Research Agent quotes only when necessary.
- Multiple Sources: The Research Agent cross-references at least 2 sources before drawing conclusions, unless the information is directly stated and non-controversial.
- Transparency: The Research Agent mentions which sources were used and explains how it arrived at conclusions.
- Accuracy Over Brevity: The Research Agent prefers detailed, technically accurate explanations over shallow summaries.
- The Research Agent admits uncertainty rather than misleading.

The Research Agent may be asked for:

- Technical explanations
- Literature reviews  
- Comparisons
- Emerging trends
- Implementation strategies

The Research Agent begins by interpreting the user's request, then:

The Research Agent breaks it into sub-questions (if applicable).

For each sub-question, the Research Agent generates a clear, concise web search query.

The Research Agent extracts and synthesizes relevant answers.

The Research Agent's output includes:

- A clear, structured answer to the user's question
- A citation list or link list of sources used

Optionally, the Research Agent provides follow-up suggestions or questions for deeper inquiry.

Here's how the Research Agent operates:

1. The Research Agent analyzes the user's request and identifies key topics to search for (3 or more), printing them out.
2. The Research Agent performs a web search for each topic.
3. The Research Agent reads and analyzes results, cross references information from multiple sources, and represents conflicting information as ranges of possible values.

Status reporting:

The Research Agent reports its status frequently using the `<status>` tags: <status>Searching for Google Founders</status>
The Research Agent reports its status in-between each tool call and before submitting results.

Last content block is the full research note -- the result of the research.
BlueYard VC Berlin early stage investments decentralization

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are an analyst that does research tasks using tools that scrape the web and create structured data.
  Structured data extraction is an experimental feature -- only enable it if the user explicitly requests it in the prompt.
  Prefer updating existing notes instead of creating new ones.
  
  <structured_mode>
    When you are done, reply with the created objects.
    Do not print the data, instead reply with inline references to the created objects.
    Those will be later substituted with the pills representing the created objects.
    Print the rest of the created objects as block references after the main note.
  
    <example>
      Based on my research, Google was founded by @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M and @dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ
  
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK464FSCKVQJAB2H662M</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW9H2ZQ</dxn></object>
      <object><dxn>dxn:queue:data:B6INSIBY3CBEF4M5VZRYBCMAHQMPYK5AJ:01K24XMVHSZHS97SG1VTVQDM5Z:01K24XPK46K31DDW62PBW92333</dxn></object>
    </example>
  </structured_mode>
  
  <unstructured_mode>
    Reply normally with the text mode of the result of your research.
  </unstructured_mode>
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

params.subject: dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNT8F
params.name: BlueYard Capital – Research Summary
params.content:
# BlueYard Capital – Research Summary

## Overview
BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based venture capital firm that invests in entrepreneurs building decentralized protocols, open infrastructure, and technologies that empower users with more control over their data and digital lives. The firm focuses on early-stage investments (primarily Seed and Series A).

## Key Details
- **Founded:** 2015
- **Headquarters:** Berlin, Germany
- **Type:** Early-stage venture capital
- **Focus Areas:** Decentralization, open-source infrastructure, data sovereignty, privacy, blockchain/Web3, developer tools, and protocol-layer technologies

## Founding Partners
- **Ciarán O'Leary** – General Partner; previously at Earlybird Venture Capital
- **Jason Whitmire** – General Partner

## Investment Thesis
BlueYard backs founders who are building foundational technologies that redistribute power — from centralized incumbents to individuals and communities. Their thesis centers on:
- **Decentralized protocols** and peer-to-peer networks
- **Open-source** software and infrastructure
- **Data ownership** and user sovereignty
- **Privacy-preserving** technologies
- Technologies that create more **open and interoperable** systems

## Notable Portfolio Companies
BlueYard has invested in a wide range of notable companies and projects, including:
- **Protocol Labs** – Creator of IPFS and Filecoin (decentralized storage)
- **Helm** (personal email/data servers)
- **Merantix** – AI venture studio
- **Unmind** – Workplace mental health platform
- **Contentful** – Headless CMS platform
- **DXOS** – Decentralized operating system and developer platform
- **Zama** – Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) for data privacy
- **Nym Technologies** – Privacy infrastructure / mixnet
- **Parity Technologies** – Blockchain infrastructure (Polkadot)

## Fund Size & Activity
BlueYard has raised multiple funds, with assets under management in the hundreds of millions of euros. They typically lead or co-lead early-stage rounds and are known for taking a long-term, conviction-driven approach to investing.

## Reputation & Approach
BlueYard is widely regarded as one of Europe's most thesis-driven venture firms. They are deeply embedded in the open-source and decentralization communities and are known for:
- Supporting founders with strong technical visions
- A patient, long-horizon investment style
- Active engagement with the broader Web3 and open-source ecosystems
- Thought leadership on topics like data rights, decentralization, and digital infrastructure

## Links
- Website: [blueyard.com](https://blueyard.com)

response.delta: power from centralized incumbents to individuals

params.name: BlueYard Capital – Research Summary
params.content:
# BlueYard Capital – Research Summary

## Overview
BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based venture capital firm that invests in entrepreneurs building decentralized protocols, open infrastructure, and technologies that empower users with more control over their data and digital lives. The firm focuses on early-stage investments (primarily Seed and Series A).

## Key Details
- **Founded:** 2015
- **Headquarters:** Berlin, Germany
- **Type:** Early-stage venture capital
- **Focus Areas:** Decentralization, open-source infrastructure, data sovereignty, privacy, blockchain/Web3, developer tools, and protocol-layer technologies

## Founding Partners
- **Ciarán O'Leary** – General Partner; previously at Earlybird Venture Capital
- **Jason Whitmire** – General Partner

## Investment Thesis
BlueYard backs founders who are building foundational technologies that redistribute power — from centralized incumbents to individuals and communities. Their thesis centers on:
- **Decentralized protocols** and peer-to-peer networks
- **Open-source** software and infrastructure
- **Data ownership** and user sovereignty
- **Privacy-preserving** technologies
- Technologies that create more **open and interoperable** systems

## Notable Portfolio Companies
BlueYard has invested in a wide range of notable companies and projects, including:
- **Protocol Labs** – Creator of IPFS and Filecoin (decentralized storage)
- **Helm** (personal email/data servers)
- **Merantix** – AI venture studio
- **Unmind** – Workplace mental health platform
- **Contentful** – Headless CMS platform
- **DXOS** – Decentralized operating system and developer platform
- **Zama** – Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) for data privacy
- **Nym Technologies** – Privacy infrastructure / mixnet
- **Parity Technologies** – Blockchain infrastructure (Polkadot)

## Fund Size & Activity
BlueYard has raised multiple funds, with assets under management in the hundreds of millions of euros. They typically lead or co-lead early-stage rounds and are known for taking a long-term, conviction-driven approach to investing.

## Reputation & Approach
BlueYard is widely regarded as one of Europe's most thesis-driven venture firms. They are deeply embedded in the open-source and decentralization communities and are known for:
- Supporting founders with strong technical visions
- A patient, long-horizon investment style
- Active engagement with the broader Web3 and open-source ecosystems
- Thought leadership on topics like data rights, decentralization, and digital infrastructure

## Links
- Website: [blueyard.com](https://blueyard.com)

params.content:
# BlueYard Capital – Research Summary

## Overview
BlueYard Capital is a Berlin-based venture capital firm that invests in entrepreneurs building decentralized protocols, open infrastructure, and technologies that empower users with more control over their data and digital lives. The firm focuses on early-stage investments (primarily Seed and Series A).

## Key Details
- **Founded:** 2015
- **Headquarters:** Berlin, Germany
- **Type:** Early-stage venture capital
- **Focus Areas:** Decentralization, open-source infrastructure, data sovereignty, privacy, blockchain/Web3, developer tools, and protocol-layer technologies

## Founding Partners
- **Ciarán O'Leary** – General Partner; previously at Earlybird Venture Capital
- **Jason Whitmire** – General Partner

## Investment Thesis
BlueYard backs founders who are building foundational technologies that redistribute power — from centralized incumbents to individuals and communities. Their thesis centers on:
- **Decentralized protocols** and peer-to-peer networks
- **Open-source** software and infrastructure
- **Data ownership** and user sovereignty
- **Privacy-preserving** technologies
- Technologies that create more **open and interoperable** systems

## Portfolio

### Decentralization & Web3
| Company | Description | Stage |
|---|---|---|
| **Protocol Labs** | Creator of IPFS and Filecoin; decentralized storage and networking protocols | Early-stage |
| **Parity Technologies** | Core infrastructure for Polkadot and Substrate blockchain frameworks | Early-stage |
| **Nym Technologies** | Privacy infrastructure using mixnet technology to protect network-level metadata | Seed |
| **Spacemesh** | Fair, decentralized proof-of-space-time (PoST) blockchain | Early-stage |
| **Radicle** | Decentralized code collaboration platform (peer-to-peer GitHub alternative) | Seed |
| **Fluence** | Decentralized serverless computing platform | Early-stage |

### Privacy & Data Sovereignty
| Company | Description | Stage |
|---|---|---|
| **Zama** | Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) tools enabling computation on encrypted data | Seed |
| **Helm** | Personal home servers for email, files, and data self-hosting | Seed |
| **DXOS** | Developer platform for building decentralized, local-first applications | Early-stage |
| **Evervault** | Encryption-as-a-service platform for developers | Early-stage |

### Developer Tools & Infrastructure
| Company | Description | Stage |
|---|---|---|
| **Contentful** | Headless CMS platform for content management at scale | Early-stage |
| **Portchain** | AI-powered port and terminal optimization | Seed |
| **Stabilogon** | Infrastructure tooling (stealth) | Seed |

### AI & Deep Tech
| Company | Description | Stage |
|---|---|---|
| **Merantix** | AI venture studio building and spinning out AI-native companies | Early-stage |
| **Luminovo** | AI-powered electronics supply chain platform | Seed |

### Health & Wellbeing
| Company | Description | Stage |
|---|---|---|
| **Unmind** | Workplace mental health and wellbeing platform | Early-stage |
| **Clue** | Female health tracking app and reproductive health research | Early-stage |

### Other Notable Investments
| Company | Description | Stage |
|---|---|---|
| **Pitch** | Collaborative presentation software (competitor to Google Slides/PowerPoint) | Early-stage |
| **Tourlane** | Personalized travel booking platform | Early-stage |
| **Useless Ethereum Token (UET)** | — | — |

### Portfolio Themes & Observations
- **Heavy decentralization tilt:** The majority of BlueYard's portfolio is oriented around Web3, blockchain infrastructure, and peer-to-peer protocols — reflecting their core thesis.
- **Privacy as a through-line:** From Zama's homomorphic encryption to Nym's mixnets and DXOS's local-first architecture, privacy-preserving technology is a unifying thread.
- **Developer-first products:** Many portfolio companies build tools and platforms aimed at developers (Contentful, DXOS, Radicle, Evervault).
- **European roots, global reach:** While headquartered in Berlin, BlueYard's portfolio spans companies across Europe, the US, and globally.
- **Notable exits/growth:** Contentful grew into one of Europe's prominent SaaS unicorns; Protocol Labs launched Filecoin, one of the largest token-based networks.

## Fund Size & Activity
BlueYard has raised multiple funds, with assets under management in the hundreds of millions of euros. They typically lead or co-lead early-stage rounds and are known for taking a long-term, conviction-driven approach to investing.

## Reputation & Approach
BlueYard is widely regarded as one of Europe's most thesis-driven venture firms. They are deeply embedded in the open-source and decentralization communities and are known for:
- Supporting founders with strong technical visions
- A patient, long-horizon investment style
- Active engagement with the broader Web3 and open-source ecosystems
- Thought leadership on topics like data rights, decentralization, and digital infrastructure

## Links
- Website: [blueyard.com](https://blueyard.com)
